---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
library(readxl)
library(jsonlite)

# Single source of truth: data/publications.xlsx (Publications, Areas, Reports,
# Meta) plus data/citations.csv (displayed Google Scholar counts, maintained by
# hand; openalex_* reference columns refreshed monthly by
# .github/workflows/refresh-citations.yml). Both figures, the research-area
# lists, and the reports section all render from these files, so they cannot
# contradict each other. Edit the workbook or the CSV, not this file, to
# change content.
pubs    <- read_excel("data/publications.xlsx", sheet = "Publications")
areas   <- read_excel("data/publications.xlsx", sheet = "Areas")
reports <- read_excel("data/publications.xlsx", sheet = "Reports")
meta_df <- read_excel("data/publications.xlsx", sheet = "Meta")
cites   <- read.csv("data/citations.csv", fileEncoding = "UTF-8",
                    colClasses = c(id = "character", cite_note = "character",
                                   openalex_id = "character",
                                   openalex_fetched = "character"))

meta <- setNames(as.character(meta_df$value), meta_df$key)

# Fail the CI render loudly rather than publish a wrong page (a failed publish
# leaves the previous version of the site live). This page states citation
# counts and a colleague's publication record; silently wrong is the worst
# failure mode it has.
stopifnot(
  # ids unique and resolvable in both files, 1:1
  !anyDuplicated(pubs$id), all(nzchar(pubs$id)),
  setequal(pubs$id, cites$id),
  # every area on a publication exists in Areas
  all(pubs$area %in% areas$key),
  # years plausible, types from the fixed vocabulary
  all(pubs$year >= 2000 & pubs$year <= 2035),
  all(pubs$type %in% c("Article", "Law review", "Book chapter", "Book")),
  # area colors are real hex colors (they go straight into SVG fills)
  all(grepl("^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$", areas$color)),
  # curated entries need a link and an order
  all(nzchar(pubs$url[pubs$featured])),
  all(!is.na(pubs$featured_order[pubs$featured])),
  # every charted row needs its bar label
  all(nzchar(pubs$short_label[pubs$id %in% cites$id[!is.na(cites$citations)]])),
  # the Meta keys the page composes text from
  all(c("citations_source", "citations_asof", "scholar_url", "tail_count",
        "tail_total", "ncsc_url", "jji_url") %in% names(meta))
)

esc <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("&", "&amp;", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("<", "&lt;", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub(">", "&gt;", x, fixed = TRUE)
  gsub('"', "&quot;", x, fixed = TRUE)
}
# Every asis chunk emits through this. The raw-HTML fence matters: without it,
# pandoc reads the chunk output as markdown, and any line indented four spaces
# becomes a <pre> code block (which is exactly what happened to the first
# draft of this page).
emit <- function(...) cat("\n```{=html}\n", ..., "\n```\n", sep = "")
# Bold the maintainer's name inside a CV-style author string.
bold_wylie <- function(x) gsub("Wylie, L.E.", "<strong>Wylie, L.E.</strong>", x, fixed = TRUE)

# ── Data for the two charts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
pubs_j <- data.frame(y = pubs$year, t = pubs$area, title = pubs$title,
                     venue = pubs$venue, type = pubs$type)

charted <- merge(pubs, cites[!is.na(cites$citations), ], by = "id")
charted <- charted[order(-charted$citations, charted$short_label), ]
cites_j <- data.frame(n = charted$citations, t = charted$area,
                      short = charted$short_label, title = charted$title,
                      venue = charted$venue, y = charted$year,
                      note = charted$cite_note)

areas_j <- data.frame(key = areas$key, label = areas$label,
                      legend = areas$legend_key, color = areas$color)

sw_json <- toJSON(list(areas = areas_j, pubs = pubs_j, cites = cites_j),
                  auto_unbox = FALSE)

# ── Computed figure text ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
y0 <- min(pubs$year); y1 <- max(pubs$year)
tl_title <- sprintf("The published record, %d&ndash;%d", y0, y1)
tl_note  <- sprintf(paste0("Peer-reviewed articles, law reviews, book chapters, ",
                           "and books, %d&ndash;%d. Full citations in the lists ",
                           "below and in the CV."), y0, y1)

n_charted  <- nrow(charted)
tail_count <- as.integer(meta["tail_count"])
tail_total <- as.integer(meta["tail_total"])
total_all  <- round((sum(charted$citations) + tail_total) / 10) * 10
num_word <- function(n) {
  words <- c("one","two","three","four","five","six","seven","eight","nine","ten",
             "eleven","twelve","thirteen","fourteen","fifteen","sixteen",
             "seventeen","eighteen","nineteen","twenty")
  if (n >= 1 && n <= 20) words[n] else as.character(n)
}
cite_title <- sprintf(paste0("The %s most-cited publications, colored by research ",
                             "area: about %d citations in all"),
                      num_word(n_charted), total_all)
merged_note <- if (any(nzchar(charted$cite_note)))
  " Duplicate Scholar records merged (counts summed)." else ""
cite_note <- sprintf(paste0("Citation counts from %s, %s.%s %s more publications ",
                            "have 9 or fewer citations each (about %d combined), ",
                            "and the newest works are not yet cited. Project ",
                            "reports and briefs are excluded; they are listed in ",
                            "the section above."),
                     esc(meta["citations_source"]), esc(meta["citations_asof"]),
                     merged_note,
                     tools::toTitleCase(num_word(tail_count)), tail_total)

# ── Research-area sections ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# The book feature keeps its bespoke layout; it renders inside the aging area.
book_html <- paste0(
'  <div class="book-feature">
    <a class="book-cover" href="https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2026-35534-000"
       target="_blank" rel="noopener">
      <img src="../pics/book-cover-aging.png"
           alt="Book cover of Aging and the Criminal Legal System by Lindsey E. Wylie and Eve M. Brank; an abstract collage in ochre, cream, lavender, and black" />
    </a>
    <div class="book-feature-body">
      <span class="project-card-tag">Book · APA, 2025</span>
      <p class="project-card-title">Aging and the Criminal Legal System</p>
      <p class="project-card-desc">
        <em>Community Services, Courts, Corrections, and Policy.</em>
        With E.M. Brank. Division 41 American Psychology-Law book series,
        American Psychological Association. Brings behavioral science to the
        legal and psychological challenges older adults face across the
        criminal legal system.
      </p>
      <a href="https://psycnet.apa.org/record/2026-35534-000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"
         style="font-weight:600; color:#8C6A8D;">
        View at APA &rarr;
      </a>
    </div>
  </div>\n')

cite_line <- function(row, linked) {
  tail <- if (row$type == "Article") "" else
    sprintf(' <span class="pub-type">(%s)</span>', tolower(row$type))
  title_html <- if (linked && nzchar(row$url)) {
    sprintf('<a class="pub-link" href="%s" target="_blank" rel="noopener">%s</a>',
            esc(row$url), esc(row$title))
  } else esc(row$title)
  sprintf("    <li>%s (%d). %s. <em>%s.</em>%s</li>\n",
          bold_wylie(esc(row$authors)), row$year, title_html, esc(row$venue), tail)
}

area_sections <- ""
for (i in seq_len(nrow(areas))) {
  a <- areas[i, ]
  in_area <- pubs[pubs$area == a$key, ]
  feat <- in_area[in_area$featured, ]
  feat <- feat[order(feat$featured_order), ]
  full <- in_area[order(-in_area$year, in_area$title), ]

  s <- sprintf('  <h3 class="pub-area-title"><span class="pub-area-dot" style="background:%s;"></span>%s</h3>\n',
               a$color, esc(a$label))
  s <- paste0(s, sprintf('  <p class="pub-area-desc">%s</p>\n', esc(a$description)))
  if (a$key == "aging") s <- paste0(s, book_html)
  s <- paste0(s, sprintf('  <ul class="pub-list" style="--area:%s;">\n', a$color))
  for (j in seq_len(nrow(feat))) s <- paste0(s, cite_line(feat[j, ], linked = TRUE))
  s <- paste0(s, "  </ul>\n")
  s <- paste0(s, sprintf('  <details class="pub-all"><summary>All %d publications in this area</summary>\n', nrow(full)))
  s <- paste0(s, sprintf('  <ul class="pub-list" style="--area:%s;">\n', a$color))
  for (j in seq_len(nrow(full))) s <- paste0(s, cite_line(full[j, ], linked = TRUE))
  s <- paste0(s, "  </ul>\n  </details>\n")
  area_sections <- paste0(area_sections, s)
}

# ── Reports section ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Every report link in the Aug 2025 CV now returns 404 (the NCSC PDF paths and
# the whole JJI squarespace asset host moved), so rows render as titles unless
# the workbook's url column carries a link that works. Org links are verified.
report_groups <- ""
for (org in unique(reports$org)) {
  in_org <- reports[reports$org == org, ]
  items <- ""
  for (j in seq_len(nrow(in_org))) {
    r <- in_org[j, ]
    t <- if (nzchar(r$url)) {
      sprintf('<a class="pub-link" href="%s" target="_blank" rel="noopener">%s</a>',
              esc(r$url), esc(r$title))
    } else esc(r$title)
    items <- paste0(items, sprintf("    <li>%s (%s).</li>\n", t, esc(r$year)))
  }
  report_groups <- paste0(report_groups, sprintf(
    '  <details class="pub-all"><summary>%s: %d reports</summary>\n  <ul class="pub-list" style="--area:#5a6b7a;">\n%s  </ul>\n  </details>\n',
    esc(org), nrow(in_org), items))
}
reports_html <- sprintf(paste0(
  '  <p class="pub-area-desc" style="margin-top:0;">Program evaluation is the applied side ',
  'of this record: process and outcome evaluations, assessment tools, and research briefs ',
  'for courts and justice agencies, at the <a href="%s" target="_blank" rel="noopener">',
  'National Center for State Courts</a> and before that directing research for the ',
  '<a href="%s" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Juvenile Justice Institute</a>\'s ',
  'Evidence-based Nebraska project. Most of these reports were published to agency sites ',
  'that have since moved or retired them, so titles are listed here and copies are ',
  'available on request.</p>\n%s'),
  esc(meta["ncsc_url"]), esc(meta["jji_url"]), report_groups)
```

```{=html}
<style>
  .pub-all { margin: 0.2rem 0 2rem; }
  .pub-all summary {
    cursor: pointer; font-weight: 600; color: #4b5563; font-size: 0.92rem;
    padding: 0.2rem 0; list-style-position: outside;
  }
  .pub-all summary:hover { color: #1f2937; }
  .pub-all[open] summary { margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
  .pub-type { color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.85em; }
</style>

<div class="page-header container">
  <p class="page-header-label">Publications</p>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">Selected Work</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">
    Every published work (articles, law reviews, book chapters, and the
    book) plotted by year and organized into four research areas. Hover
    any point for the citation; highlights follow.
  </p>
</div>
```

```{r inject-data}
#| results: asis
#| echo: false
emit('<script>window.SW = ', sw_json, ';</script>\n',
     '<div class="container" style="max-width: 960px;">\n',
     '  <div id="pubchart-card" class="chart-card">\n',
     '    <p class="chart-card-tag">Figure · Timeline</p>\n',
     '    <p class="chart-card-title">', tl_title, '</p>\n',
     '    <div id="pubchart" style="overflow-x:auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch;"></div>\n',
     '    <p class="chart-card-note">\n',
     '      <span id="pubchart-swipe-hint" style="display:none;">Swipe the chart to see the full timeline. </span>\n',
     '      ', tl_note, '\n',
     '    </p>\n',
     '  </div>\n',
     '</div>')
```

```{=html}
<div id="pub-tooltip" style="position:absolute; display:none; pointer-events:none; z-index:50;
  background:#3C3C3C; color:#ffffff; border-radius:6px; padding:0.55rem 0.7rem;
  font-size:0.8rem; line-height:1.45; max-width:320px; box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.18);"></div>

<script>
(function () {
  // Threads and publications come from the workbook via window.SW, so this
  // figure cannot disagree with the lists below or the citations figure.
  const THREADS = SW.areas.map(a => ({ key: a.key, label: a.label, color: a.color }));
  const PUBS = SW.pubs;

  const years = PUBS.map(p => p.y);
  const Y0 = Math.min(...years), Y1 = Math.max(...years);
  const W = 920, PAD_L = 16, PAD_R = 16, PAD_T = 8;
  const LANE_LABEL_H = 32, TICK_H = 30;
  const DOT_R = 6, STACK_GAP = 17;

  const ns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg";
  const el = (n, attrs) => {
    const e = document.createElementNS(ns, n);
    for (const k in attrs) e.setAttribute(k, attrs[k]);
    return e;
  };
  const xFor = y => PAD_L + ((y - Y0) / (Y1 - Y0)) * (W - PAD_L - PAD_R);

  // lane heights sized to the deepest stack in each thread
  const stacks = {};
  PUBS.forEach(p => {
    const k = p.t + p.y;
    stacks[k] = (stacks[k] || 0) + 1;
    p.stackIdx = stacks[k] - 1;
  });
  const laneMax = {};
  THREADS.forEach(t => {
    laneMax[t.key] = Math.max(1, ...Object.keys(stacks)
      .filter(k => k.startsWith(t.key)).map(k => stacks[k]));
  });

  let laneY = PAD_T;
  const lanes = {};
  THREADS.forEach(t => {
    const h = LANE_LABEL_H + laneMax[t.key] * STACK_GAP + 8;
    lanes[t.key] = { top: laneY, height: h, ...t };
    laneY += h;
  });
  const H = laneY + TICK_H;

  const svg = el("svg", { viewBox: `0 0 ${W} ${H}`, width: "100%", role: "group",
    "aria-label": "Timeline of publications by year and research area" });
  svg.style.fontFamily = "inherit";
  // below this width the chart scrolls horizontally instead of shrinking,
  // so the text stays legible on phones
  svg.style.minWidth = "680px";
  svg.style.display = "block";

  // hairline year gridlines + ticks (solid, recessive)
  for (let y = Y0; y <= Y1; y++) {
    const x = xFor(y);
    svg.appendChild(el("line", { x1: x, y1: PAD_T, x2: x, y2: laneY, stroke: "#f0ece7", "stroke-width": 1 }));
    if (y % 2 === 1) {
      const tick = el("text", { x: x, y: laneY + 20, "text-anchor": "middle", fill: "#777", "font-size": 13.5 });
      tick.style.fontVariantNumeric = "tabular-nums";
      tick.textContent = y;
      svg.appendChild(tick);
    }
  }

  // lane separators + labels (the identity channel — text in ink, swatch carries color)
  THREADS.forEach((t, i) => {
    const L = lanes[t.key];
    if (i > 0) svg.appendChild(el("line", { x1: PAD_L, y1: L.top, x2: W - PAD_R, y2: L.top, stroke: "#eee9e4", "stroke-width": 1 }));
    svg.appendChild(el("circle", { cx: PAD_L + 6, cy: L.top + 16, r: 6, fill: t.color }));
    const lbl = el("text", { x: PAD_L + 19, y: L.top + 21, fill: "#3C3C3C", "font-size": 16, "font-weight": 600 });
    lbl.textContent = t.label;
    svg.appendChild(lbl);
  });

  // dots: series color fill + 2px surface ring; 24px transparent hit target
  const tip = document.getElementById("pub-tooltip");
  const showTip = (p, target) => {
    tip.replaceChildren();
    const strong = document.createElement("strong");
    strong.textContent = p.title;
    const meta = document.createElement("div");
    meta.style.cssText = "color:#d9d4cf; margin-top:0.2rem;";
    meta.textContent = `${p.venue} · ${p.y} · ${p.type}`;
    tip.append(strong, meta);
    const r = target.getBoundingClientRect();
    tip.style.display = "block";
    const tw = tip.offsetWidth;
    let left = window.scrollX + r.left + r.width / 2 - tw / 2;
    left = Math.max(8, Math.min(left, window.scrollX + document.documentElement.clientWidth - tw - 8));
    tip.style.left = left + "px";
    tip.style.top = (window.scrollY + r.top - tip.offsetHeight - 10) + "px";
  };
  const hideTip = () => { tip.style.display = "none"; };

  PUBS.forEach(p => {
    const L = lanes[p.t];
    const cx = xFor(p.y);
    const cy = L.top + L.height - 12 - p.stackIdx * STACK_GAP;
    svg.appendChild(el("circle", { cx, cy, r: DOT_R, fill: L.color, stroke: "#ffffff", "stroke-width": 2 }));
    const hit = el("circle", { cx, cy, r: 12, fill: "transparent", tabindex: 0, role: "img",
      "aria-label": `${p.title}. ${p.venue}, ${p.y}. ${p.type}.` });
    hit.style.cursor = "pointer";
    hit.addEventListener("pointerenter", () => showTip(p, hit));
    hit.addEventListener("pointerleave", hideTip);
    hit.addEventListener("focus", () => showTip(p, hit));
    hit.addEventListener("blur", hideTip);
    svg.appendChild(hit);
  });

  const chartBox = document.getElementById("pubchart");
  chartBox.appendChild(svg);
  const hint = document.getElementById("pubchart-swipe-hint");
  const updateHint = () => {
    hint.style.display = chartBox.scrollWidth > chartBox.clientWidth ? "inline" : "none";
  };
  updateHint();
  window.addEventListener("resize", updateHint);
})();
</script>
```

```{=html}
<div class="section-divider" aria-hidden="true">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 220 34" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false">
    <defs>
      <linearGradient id="divider-line-sw1" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
        <stop offset="0" stop-color="#8C6A8D"/>
        <stop offset="1" stop-color="#E98973"/>
      </linearGradient>
    </defs>
    <path d="M 6 28 C 60 26, 120 18, 214 6" fill="none" stroke="url(#divider-line-sw1)" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <circle cx="22" cy="24" r="3.2" fill="#8C6A8D"/>
    <circle cx="58" cy="28" r="3.2" fill="#9d7397"/>
    <circle cx="96" cy="20" r="3.2" fill="#b07d90"/>
    <circle cx="132" cy="22" r="3.2" fill="#c48389"/>
    <circle cx="168" cy="12" r="3.2" fill="#d78681"/>
    <circle cx="204" cy="10" r="3.2" fill="#E98973"/>
  </svg>
</div>
```

## Research Areas {.section-heading}

```{r area-sections}
#| results: asis
#| echo: false
emit('<div class="container" style="max-width: 820px;">\n', area_sections, '</div>')
```

```{=html}
<div class="section-divider" aria-hidden="true">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 220 34" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false">
    <defs>
      <linearGradient id="divider-line-sw2" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
        <stop offset="0" stop-color="#8C6A8D"/>
        <stop offset="1" stop-color="#E98973"/>
      </linearGradient>
    </defs>
    <path d="M 6 28 C 60 26, 120 18, 214 6" fill="none" stroke="url(#divider-line-sw2)" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <circle cx="22" cy="24" r="3.2" fill="#8C6A8D"/>
    <circle cx="58" cy="28" r="3.2" fill="#9d7397"/>
    <circle cx="96" cy="20" r="3.2" fill="#b07d90"/>
    <circle cx="132" cy="22" r="3.2" fill="#c48389"/>
    <circle cx="168" cy="12" r="3.2" fill="#d78681"/>
    <circle cx="204" cy="10" r="3.2" fill="#E98973"/>
  </svg>
</div>
```

## Program Evaluations and Applied Research {.section-heading}

```{r reports-section}
#| results: asis
#| echo: false
emit('<div class="container" style="max-width: 820px;">\n', reports_html, '</div>')
```

```{=html}
<div class="section-divider" aria-hidden="true">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 220 34" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false">
    <defs>
      <linearGradient id="divider-line-sw4" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
        <stop offset="0" stop-color="#8C6A8D"/>
        <stop offset="1" stop-color="#E98973"/>
      </linearGradient>
    </defs>
    <path d="M 6 28 C 60 26, 120 18, 214 6" fill="none" stroke="url(#divider-line-sw4)" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <circle cx="22" cy="24" r="3.2" fill="#8C6A8D"/>
    <circle cx="58" cy="28" r="3.2" fill="#9d7397"/>
    <circle cx="96" cy="20" r="3.2" fill="#b07d90"/>
    <circle cx="132" cy="22" r="3.2" fill="#c48389"/>
    <circle cx="168" cy="12" r="3.2" fill="#d78681"/>
    <circle cx="204" cy="10" r="3.2" fill="#E98973"/>
  </svg>
</div>
```

## Citation Impact {.section-heading}

```{r cite-card}
#| results: asis
#| echo: false
emit('<div class="container" style="max-width: 960px;">\n',
     '  <div id="citechart-card" class="chart-card">\n',
     '    <p class="chart-card-tag">Figure · Citations</p>\n',
     '    <p class="chart-card-title">', cite_title, '</p>\n',
     '    <div id="citechart" style="overflow-x:auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch;"></div>\n',
     '    <p class="chart-card-note">\n',
     '      <span id="citechart-swipe-hint" style="display:none;">Swipe the chart to see full bars. </span>\n',
     '      ', cite_note, '\n',
     '    </p>\n',
     '  </div>\n',
     '</div>')
```

```{=html}
<script>
(function () {
  // Same workbook data as the timeline: SW.areas keys the legend, SW.cites
  // carries the bars, already sorted by count.
  const THREADS = {};
  SW.areas.forEach(a => { THREADS[a.key] = { label: a.label, key: a.legend, color: a.color }; });
  const CITES = SW.cites;

  const W = 920, LABEL_W = 258, VAL_W = 52, PAD_T = 34, ROW_H = 27, BAR_H = 15;
  const H = PAD_T + CITES.length * ROW_H + 30;
  const MAXV = Math.max(...CITES.map(d => d.n));
  const x0 = LABEL_W + 8, x1 = W - VAL_W;
  const xFor = v => x0 + (v / MAXV) * (x1 - x0);

  const ns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg";
  const el = (n, attrs) => {
    const e = document.createElementNS(ns, n);
    for (const k in attrs) e.setAttribute(k, attrs[k]);
    return e;
  };

  const svg = el("svg", { viewBox: `0 0 ${W} ${H}`, width: "100%", role: "group",
    "aria-label": "Bar chart of citations for the most-cited publications" });
  svg.style.fontFamily = "inherit";
  svg.style.minWidth = "680px";
  svg.style.display = "block";

  // legend row (identity channel for the four threads)
  let lx = 8;
  Object.values(THREADS).forEach(t => {
    svg.appendChild(el("rect", { x: lx, y: 6, width: 13, height: 13, rx: 3, fill: t.color }));
    const lt = el("text", { x: lx + 19, y: 17, fill: "#555", "font-size": 13.5 });
    lt.textContent = t.key;
    svg.appendChild(lt);
    lx += 19 + t.key.length * 7.1 + 26;
  });

  // gridlines every 50 (solid hairlines, recessive), sized to the data
  for (let v = 50; v < MAXV; v += 50) {
    const gx = xFor(v);
    svg.appendChild(el("line", { x1: gx, y1: PAD_T - 4, x2: gx, y2: H - 26, stroke: "#f0ece7", "stroke-width": 1 }));
    const gt = el("text", { x: gx, y: H - 10, "text-anchor": "middle", fill: "#999", "font-size": 12.5 });
    gt.style.fontVariantNumeric = "tabular-nums";
    gt.textContent = v;
    svg.appendChild(gt);
  }

  const tip = document.getElementById("pub-tooltip");
  const showTip = (d, target) => {
    tip.replaceChildren();
    const strong = document.createElement("strong");
    strong.textContent = `${d.n} citations`;
    const ttl = document.createElement("div");
    ttl.style.cssText = "margin-top:0.2rem;";
    ttl.textContent = d.title;
    const meta = document.createElement("div");
    meta.style.cssText = "color:#d9d4cf; margin-top:0.2rem;";
    meta.textContent = `${d.venue} · ${d.y} · ${THREADS[d.t].label}` + (d.note ? ` · ${d.note}` : "");
    tip.append(strong, ttl, meta);
    const r = target.getBoundingClientRect();
    tip.style.display = "block";
    const tw = tip.offsetWidth;
    let left = window.scrollX + r.left + r.width / 2 - tw / 2;
    left = Math.max(8, Math.min(left, window.scrollX + document.documentElement.clientWidth - tw - 8));
    tip.style.left = left + "px";
    tip.style.top = (window.scrollY + r.top - tip.offsetHeight - 8) + "px";
  };
  const hideTip = () => { tip.style.display = "none"; };

  CITES.forEach((d, i) => {
    const y = PAD_T + i * ROW_H;
    const by = y + (ROW_H - BAR_H) / 2;
    const bw = xFor(d.n) - x0;

    const lbl = el("text", { x: LABEL_W, y: by + BAR_H - 3, "text-anchor": "end", fill: "#3C3C3C", "font-size": 13.5 });
    lbl.textContent = d.short;
    svg.appendChild(lbl);

    // bar: square at baseline, 4px rounded data-end
    const r = Math.min(4, bw);
    const bar = el("path", { d: `M ${x0} ${by} L ${x0 + bw - r} ${by} Q ${x0 + bw} ${by} ${x0 + bw} ${by + r} L ${x0 + bw} ${by + BAR_H - r} Q ${x0 + bw} ${by + BAR_H} ${x0 + bw - r} ${by + BAR_H} L ${x0} ${by + BAR_H} Z`,
      fill: THREADS[d.t].color });
    svg.appendChild(bar);

    const val = el("text", { x: x0 + bw + 8, y: by + BAR_H - 3, fill: "#3C3C3C", "font-size": 13, "font-weight": 600 });
    val.style.fontVariantNumeric = "tabular-nums";
    val.textContent = d.n;
    svg.appendChild(val);

    // full-row hit target (>24px tall) with keyboard focus
    const hit = el("rect", { x: 0, y: y, width: W, height: ROW_H, fill: "transparent", tabindex: 0, role: "img",
      "aria-label": `${d.title}. ${d.venue}, ${d.y}. ${d.n} citations.` });
    hit.style.cursor = "pointer";
    hit.addEventListener("pointerenter", () => { bar.setAttribute("opacity", "0.75"); showTip(d, hit); });
    hit.addEventListener("pointerleave", () => { bar.removeAttribute("opacity"); hideTip(); });
    hit.addEventListener("focus", () => { bar.setAttribute("opacity", "0.75"); showTip(d, hit); });
    hit.addEventListener("blur", () => { bar.removeAttribute("opacity"); hideTip(); });
    svg.appendChild(hit);
  });

  const box = document.getElementById("citechart");
  box.appendChild(svg);
  const hint = document.getElementById("citechart-swipe-hint");
  const updateHint = () => {
    hint.style.display = box.scrollWidth > box.clientWidth ? "inline" : "none";
  };
  updateHint();
  window.addEventListener("resize", updateHint);
})();
</script>
```

```{=html}
<div class="section-divider" aria-hidden="true">
  <svg viewBox="0 0 220 34" aria-hidden="true" focusable="false">
    <defs>
      <linearGradient id="divider-line-sw3" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="0">
        <stop offset="0" stop-color="#8C6A8D"/>
        <stop offset="1" stop-color="#E98973"/>
      </linearGradient>
    </defs>
    <path d="M 6 28 C 60 26, 120 18, 214 6" fill="none" stroke="url(#divider-line-sw3)" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <circle cx="22" cy="24" r="3.2" fill="#8C6A8D"/>
    <circle cx="58" cy="28" r="3.2" fill="#9d7397"/>
    <circle cx="96" cy="20" r="3.2" fill="#b07d90"/>
    <circle cx="132" cy="22" r="3.2" fill="#c48389"/>
    <circle cx="168" cy="12" r="3.2" fill="#d78681"/>
    <circle cx="204" cy="10" r="3.2" fill="#E98973"/>
  </svg>
</div>
```

## Curriculum Vitae {.section-heading}

```{=html}
<div class="container" style="max-width: 820px;">
  <p style="color:#4b5563;">
    The full CV includes all peer-reviewed publications, book chapters, law
    reviews, funded grants, project reports, presentations, and courses
    taught.
  </p>

  <a class="cv-download" href="../CV/Wylie%20CV%207.2026.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
    <span class="cv-download-icon">↓</span> Download CV <span class="cv-download-ext">PDF</span>
  </a>

  <p style="margin-top:2rem; color:#6b7280; font-size:0.95rem;">
    Full publication list on
    <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=-EyTKLcAAAAJ&amp;hl=en" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Google Scholar</a>.
  </p>
</div>
```
